steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the land is "with some exaggeration called a garden," meaning that label is a stretch. The flower beds "confirmed" that illusion — they supplied visible evidence that made the exaggerated label feel real. The replacement word must mean "provided supporting evidence for."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Necessitated': Means "made necessary." Flower beds do not make the garden label necessary; they support it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Substantiated': Means "gave substance or proof to." The blooming pansies substantiated the garden illusion by providing tangible, visible evidence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Designated': Means "formally named or assigned." The flower beds do not officially name anything. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Promised': Means "gave reason to expect a future outcome." The confirmation here is present, not forward-looking. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
